prompt.body:
## Goal
The factory holds every selectable model in one operator-maintained registry (data, not code), with a stdlib loader that resolves a role from layered sources, enforces the rank gate, and validates its own consistency — with no change to the runner yet.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] THE SYSTEM SHALL provide a registry file at the factory repo root declaring, per model entry, a selection name (the key), a CLI id, a provider, an integer rank, and a quota pool.
- [ ] THE SYSTEM SHALL declare in that file a default entry per role for `build` and `review`, and SHALL ship entries named `sonnet` (id `claude-sonnet-5`) and `opus` (id `claude-opus-4-8`) so existing issues and manifests keep working unchanged.
- [ ] THE SYSTEM SHALL default `review` to a model of strictly higher rank than `build`.
- [ ] THE SYSTEM SHALL provide a loader that reads the registry from the factory's own deployed checkout (the file beside the loader), never from a git ref.
- [ ] WHEN asked to resolve a role from a task value, a manifest value, and an env value, THE SYSTEM SHALL apply precedence per-task over per-repo over the registry's per-role default.
- [ ] THE SYSTEM SHALL expose a rank check that holds only WHEN the review entry's rank is strictly greater than the build entry's rank AND both share one provider AND both are ranked.
- [ ] WHEN the registry is validated, THE SYSTEM SHALL fail validation IF the default build/review pair does not satisfy the rank check, OR a stage-tier reference names a missing entry, OR a stage tier exceeds the build rank, OR two entries in one provider share a rank, OR any entry omits id/provider/rank/quota_pool.
- [ ] THE SYSTEM SHALL record the upper-pipeline model convention (strategy / product definition / authoring on the strongest available class; execution delegated down-tier) as a header comment in the registry file.
- [ ] THE SYSTEM SHALL validate the shipped registry successfully (a self-consistency test that a bad registry PR would fail).
- [ ] THE SYSTEM SHALL leave `tools/dev-runner.sh` unchanged (this slice ships data + loader + tests only).

## Context & links
Create the model registry as versioned data in the **factory** repo plus a stdlib loader the runner will later consume. A new `models.toml` at the factory repo root declares, per entry, a selection **name** (the key), the CLI **id**, the **provider**, an integer **rank** (ordered only within a provider), and a **quota_pool**; plus `[roles]` defaults `build` and `review`; plus an optional `[roles.stage_tiers]` mapping `check_repair`/`review_repair` to cheaper entries. Ship entries `sonnet` (id `claude-sonnet-5`, rank 30, pool `anthropic-main`) and `opus` (id `claude-opus-4-8`, rank 40, pool `anthropic-main`), defaults `build=sonnet`, `review=opus`. The header comment records the upper-pipeline operating convention. Put the loader under `tools/` as a `registry.py` — stdlib `tomllib` only, matching the shared-helper style of `tools/textutil.py` (the repo's only dep is pytest, per `requirements-dev.txt`). It exposes: load; **validate** (the self-consistency rules above); **resolve** a role from (task value, manifest value, env value) with precedence per-task > per-repo > registry default; and a **rank check** (strict `review.rank > build.rank`, same provider, both ranked). It also offers a JSON CLI (`resolve …`, `validate`) the runner will shell to — the same shell-to-python3 seam the runner already uses for the target-repo manifest at `tools/dev-runner.sh:87-96`. The loader reads the registry from the factory's own deployed checkout (the file next to it), never a git ref — the deliberate exception (the factory's working tree *is* its deployment, the same trust already extended to `tools/dev-runner.sh`). A new `test_registry.py` under `tests/` (pytest, like the rest of the suite) loads the *shipped* `models.toml` and asserts self-consistency, and unit-tests resolution precedence + the rank check. **Do not touch `tools/dev-runner.sh`** — wiring is a later task. Keep the loader dependency-free and tolerant of unknown keys so later additive fields (per-role `effort`, `auto_merge`) don't break it.

## Test expectations
The independent tester derives tests from the criteria: a self-consistency test over the shipped registry (default pair passes the rank check; every entry complete; ranks distinct within a provider; stage-tier refs exist and don't exceed build rank); resolution-precedence tests (task beats repo beats default); rank-check tests (strict, same-provider, both-ranked; an unranked or inverted or cross-provider pair fails); and a guard that the shipped registry validates. Tests live under `tests/` and run under the factory check command `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/ -q`.

## Constraints / out of scope
No change to `tools/dev-runner.sh`. No new pip dependency (stdlib only). Do not implement per-role `effort` (deferred this iteration; just don't reject the key). Env-var override *names* for the runner are a later task's concern — this task only provides the resolve/validate/rank-check library and the data.

## Size
M — one PR, careful

---
